perturbation.original_text: During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity").
perturbation.changed_text: During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") may, but is not obligated to, provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to provide' to 'may, but is not obligated to, provide' introduces a contradiction. Initially, the contract stipulates that each party must provide the right of first offer. This perturbation transforms that obligation into an option, contradicting the original intent of section 1. This creates uncertainty regarding whether a party is legally required to offer development opportunities, impacting enforcement. It could also contradict section 9, which generally enforces the agreement terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Failure of either Party at any time to require the performance by the other Party of any provision hereof shall in no way affect the full right to require such performance at any time thereafter. Hyatt and Playa will not waive or impair any right, power, or option this Agreement reserves because of any custom or practice that varies from this Agreement's terms; Hyatt's or Playa's failure, refusal, or neglect to exercise any right under this Agreement or to insist upon the other's compliance with this Agreement; Hyatt's or Playa's waiver of or failure to exercise any right, power, or option, whether of the same, similar, or different nature, with other Hyatt All-Inclusive Resorts or any other agreements between the parties and/or their affiliates; or Hyatt's or Playa's acceptance of any payments due from the other Party after any breach of this Agreement (unless such payments are made within any applicable cure periods).
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates each party provides the right of first offer. The changed text makes it optional ('may, but is not obligated to'). This directly contradicts the 'full right to require such performance' clause in the contradicted text. Making the offer optional undermines the enforceability of the original agreement, as a party could now choose not to perform, conflicting with the other party's right to demand performance.

perturbation.original_text: All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute, and, failing such amicable settlement, shall be finally settled by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules").
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties; however, neither party is obligated to enter into negotiation. Should negotiation occur and fail, disputes may be settled at either of the parties discretion by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules").
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may' and adding that 'neither party is obligated to enter into negotiation', the mandatory nature of the initial negotiation period is removed. This creates a contradiction because the original text implies a required attempt at amicable settlement before arbitration, which is now optional. It makes the first step in dispute resolution of fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice, not compulsory and leaves uncertainty on whether the rules for arbitration will be required, if negotitation is not commenced. This directly undermines the original intent of Section 5(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dispute Resolution. (a) All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days from the date of written notice by either Party of the existence of such dispute, and, failing such amicable settlement, shall be finally settled by arbitration under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce ("Rules"). To the extent there is any conflict between the Rules and the Federal Arbitration Act as it pertains to such arbitration, the Rules shall prevail. (b) The arbitration panel shall consist of: (i) one arbitrator in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), and the aggregate damages sought by the counter-claimant are stated to be less than Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000); or 4 (ii) three arbitrators in the event the aggregate damages sought by the claimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000), or the aggregate damages sought by the counterclaimant are stated to be equal to or exceed Five Hundred Thousand US Dollars (US$500,000). Each arbitrator (1) shall have no fewer than ten (10) years' experience in the international hotel business, (2) shall be licensed to practice law in the United States, and (3) shall not be a person, or an affiliate of a person, who has any past, present or currently contemplated future business or personal relationship with either Playa, Hyatt or any of their respective affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the mandatory negotiation period, replacing 'shall' with 'may' and explicitly stating that 'neither party is obligated to enter into negotiation.' This directly contradicts section (a) of the 'Dispute Resolution' clause, which states that disputes 'shall to the extent possible be settled amicably by negotiation between the Parties within fifteen (15) days.' The changed text makes negotiation optional, while the original text mandates it before arbitration. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall not be supplemented or amended except by an instrument in writing executed and delivered by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement may be supplemented or amended by either an instrument in writing executed and delivered by both Parties, or by verbal agreement between the Parties where documentation is not immediately available. Any verbal amendments agreed must be documented within 30 days to be effective permanently.
perturbation.explanation: The change to section 8 introducing the 'verbal agreement' amendment option directly contradicts the original requirement for a written instrument signed by both parties. This alteration brings in uncertainty about how amendments can be made validly, potentially causing disputes over whether a verbal agreement is considered a valid amendment if not documented, even though the original text specifies written amendments. It creates a conflict between the formal, written amendment requirement and the new, informal, verbal agreement option, as section 6 states, this agreement supersedes all prior understadings and writings not contained in this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior understandings and writings between the Parties. No Party may rely on any alleged oral or written understandings, agreements, or representations not contained in this Agreement. Any policies that either Party adopts and implements from time to time to guide them in their decision-making are subject to change, are not a part of this Agreement, and are not binding on them.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the possibility of verbal agreements amending the contract, which directly contradicts the 'Entire Agreement' clause stating that the agreement supersedes all prior understandings and writings not contained within the agreement and that no party may rely on oral understandings. This creates a direct conflict as the original agreement requires all changes to be in writing, while the modified section allows for verbal amendments, thus violating the explicit terms of the 'Entire Agreement' clause.
